history.title: Fresh look — "Daily Streak" rebrand (PR #1)
changes: New visual identity built from a 15-product competitive study (UWorld, Duolingo, Brilliant, Khan, PrepFE, PPI and more): a brighter, friendlier daily-habit look that leans into what makes us different — the streak + spaced-repetition habit loop no $1,500 FE course has

Light theme is now the default for easier long study sessions and dense Handbook/equation reading; the original navy dark theme is a one-tap toggle in the top bar (your choice is remembered, and first visit follows your device setting)

New type + color system: chunky Bricolage Grotesque headlines, highly-legible Plus Jakarta Sans body, JetBrains Mono for IDs and Handbook page refs; the wordmark now reads PEFE (amber) + Prep — every color pair checked for WCAG-AA contrast

Accessibility + mobile pass: visible keyboard focus rings, reduced-motion support, correct/incorrect shown with ✓/✗ glyphs (not color alone), 44px touch targets, and the top nav collapses to a thumb-friendly bottom tab bar on phones

Foundation only (PR #1): look, themes, accessibility and the mobile shell — no questions or data changed. Bigger layout upgrades (a 'where you stand' Today dashboard, per-distractor explanations, a custom test builder, a readiness score) are next, per docs/rebrand-research.md

history.title: Day 10 added — Fundamental Principles
changes: Fundamental Principles (Day 10, 18 questions) added — KA 5 subtopics A–C: population projections & demand (linear/algebraic, geometric/log/exponential, percent, ratio-correlation, decreasing-rate-of-increase/saturation models); reactors (CMFR and PFR first-order HRT comparison, zero-order CMFR HRT, CMFR second-order effluent concentration, CMFR vs PFR efficiency comparison); materials science (engineering stress, true stress, Hooke's Law/Young's modulus SI and USCS, thermal deformation δt = αL(T−T0), thermal expansion strain ε = αΔT, fire hydrant rated capacity at 20 psi, fire hydrant discharge to atmosphere)
Bank now holds 222 questions spanning Days 1–10; every numeric answer recomputed and verified in Python

Cheat sheet extended with 15 new Fundamental Principles equations (all 5 population models, 4 reactor formulas, 4 materials science formulas, 2 fire hydrant NFPA 291 formulas); printable Day-10 study PDF to be generated with pdflatex

All Day-10 formulas cross-checked against NCEES FE Reference Handbook v10.6 via text extract (Google Drive): population projection equations p.334, CMFR/PFR steady-state reactor table pp.330–331, engineering/true stress and Hooke's Law p.121, thermal expansion coefficient p.127, thermal deformations p.131, fire hydrant NFPA 291 formulas pp.304–305

KA 5 (7–11 exam questions) fully covered: all 3 subtopics A (5 population models + 2 fire flow), B (4 reactor types/orders), C (6 materials science) — SI + USCS mix (Q13 USCS strain, Q17–18 USCS gpm/psi)

history.title: Full cross-device sync — streak, stars & achievements included
changes: Everything now follows your account across devices: spaced-repetition review state, attempt history, your daily streak, ⭐ My List stars, achievements, focus time, and settings — not just your plan reviews

How it works: your device keeps using fast local storage, but while you're signed in the site hydrates from your account on load and pushes changes back automatically (debounced, and on leaving the page) — sign in on a phone and laptop and they stay in lockstep

Two-way merge keeps the best of each device: the higher streak, the union of your stars and achievements, and the most-advanced review box per question, so nothing is lost when you study in more than one place
Backed by a new per-user state store in Supabase (RLS-protected); the one-time import banner is replaced by always-on sync that shows a live 'synced' status on Account and My Plan

history.title: Import your on-device progress into your account
changes: New one-click 'Sync your on-device progress' banner on the Account and My Plan pages: when you sign in, any study progress saved on this device (spaced-repetition review state + attempt history) imports into your account so your synced plan and reviews pick up where you left off
Safe to run on any device — it only adds questions you haven't already started in your account and won't double-count attempts, so signing in on a second device can't clobber or inflate your stats
Your on-device exam date carries over to your account profile during the import

history.title: Handbook page numbers on every static-bank question
changes: Every question in the daily Bank (Days 1–9, 204 questions) now cites its exact NCEES FE Reference Handbook v10.6 page in the Handbook reference line — e.g. 'Manning's Equation (p. 303)', 'Reynolds Number (p. 186)', 'Lime-Soda Softening (p. 347)' — so you can jump straight to the formula on exam day

Pages were resolved against the real Handbook v10.6 (the searchable PDF used on the exam): each was cross-checked against the Handbook's own index and the section text, and disambiguated by chapter so look-alike terms land on the right page (e.g. open-channel Manning p. 303, not the pipe-flow reference)

The handful of questions answerable from fundamentals rather than a printed Handbook formula (simultaneous equations, Pascal's hydraulic press, return-period/flood-frequency T = 1/P) are now labeled 'fundamental' instead of being given a misleading page
Live Bank questions are unaffected by this change; this updates the static daily day-files only

history.title: Live Bank, accounts & training plans (Supabase platform)
changes: Live Bank — a new database-backed question bank holding 762 Handbook-verified questions across all 15 FE Environmental knowledge areas, filterable by area, type, and source, served live from Supabase (the daily static day-sets stay separate and unchanged)

Accounts + personalized training plans: sign in with Google, pick a 2-week to 3-month plan that counts down to your exam date — covering every area in official exam proportions, with a weekly mini-exam and a final 110-question mock; per-question progress and spaced-repetition reviews save to your account

Report a question: flag any question for review (incorrect answer, typo, ambiguous wording, wrong equation/Handbook reference, off-topic, or free-text) from every question card — Live Bank, Bank, Today, and dashboard
Bank cards now show the question and options first; the correct answer and worked formula reveal only when you choose 'Show answer', so you can attempt the question first
The bank grew from the day-file sets to 762 questions by extracting and Handbook-verifying every FE Environmental practice problem and course-note across the source library

history.title: Day 9 added — Environmental Chemistry
changes: Environmental Chemistry (Day 9, 20 questions) added — KA 6 subtopics A–D: stoichiometry/reactions (pH, pKa, Ksp, alkalinity, ThOD), kinetics (first-order decay, half-life, zero-order rate law), organic chemistry (functional groups, degree of unsaturation, alkane formula, chlorinated solvents PCE/TCE), multimedia equilibrium partitioning (Henry's Law, Kow, BCF, Koc, retardation factor, contaminant velocity)
Bank now holds 204 questions spanning Days 1–9; every numeric answer recomputed and verified in Python

Cheat sheet extended with 11 new Environmental Chemistry equations (pH, H-H buffer, alkalinity as CaCO3, Ksp, zero-order rate law, DoU, alkane formula, Henry's Law Hcc, Kow, BCF, Koc/Kd); printable Day-9 study PDF generated with pdflatex (materials/)

All Day-9 formulas cross-checked against NCEES FE Reference Handbook v10.6 via Google Drive subagent; confirmed: Ksp, first-order decay, zero-order rate law, Henry's Law Hcc, retardation factor R, BCF all in Handbook; Henderson-Hasselbalch NOT in Handbook (provided in Q03 stem, tagged in-stem)
KA 6 (7–11 exam questions) fully covered: all 4 subtopics A–D represented — SI + USCS mix (Q17 SI retardation, Q18 USCS ft/day transport, Q19 USCS ft³ volumes)

history.title: Day 8 added — Water & Wastewater II
changes: Water & Wastewater II (Day 8, 20 questions) added — ThOD stoichiometry, BOD/COD biodegradability ratio, return sludge mass balance, WAS flow from SRT, primary TSS removal loading, secondary clarifier sizing, NRC recirculation factor, chlorine demand, lime-soda softening Reaction 4 concept and dose, PFR first-order, biotower with recycle, biotower temperature correction (θ=1.035), biological denitrification methanol dose, F:M ratio vs process type, anaerobic digestion gas production and SRT from design table, aerobic digestion O2 demand, annual potable water offset from reclaimed water, dual-distribution reuse fraction
Bank now holds 184 questions spanning Days 1–8; every numeric answer recomputed and verified in Python

Cheat sheet extended with 10 new Water & Wastewater II equations (RAS/WAS formulas, biotower with recycle, NRC factor F, methanol denitrification, AD/aerobic digestion criteria); printable Day-8 study PDF generated with pdflatex (materials/)
All Day-8 formulas cross-checked against NCEES FE Reference Handbook v10.6 text (pp. 327–353) via Google Drive subagent; confirmed Handbook coverage for each tagged formula

KA 12 (12–18 exam questions) continued: all 7 subtopics A–G represented — SI + USCS mix; Day 8 adds depth to biological processes (biotower recycle, denitrification, F:M, PFR) and sludge treatment (aerobic/anaerobic digestion design criteria)

history.title: Day 7 added — Water & Wastewater I
changes: Water & Wastewater I (Day 7, 21 questions) added — hardness as CaCO3, BOD5 test, mass loading, CMFR reactor, clarifier overflow rate & HRT, Stokes' Law settling, CT disinfection, lime-soda softening, alum coagulation, BOD exertion, ultimate BOD, temperature correction, activated sludge MLSS, SVI, biotower fixed-film, sludge volume, aerobic digestion SRT, per capita BOD loading, water conservation
Bank now holds 164 questions spanning Days 1–7; every numeric answer recomputed and verified in Python
Cheat sheet extended with 15 new Water & Wastewater equations; printable Day-7 study PDF generated with pdflatex (materials/)
All Day-7 formulas cross-checked against the NCEES FE Reference Handbook v10.6 text (pp. 327–353)
KA 12 (12–18 exam questions) coverage begun: subtopics A–G all represented — SI + USCS mix throughout

history.title: Day 6 added — Groundwater, Soils & Sediments
changes: Groundwater, Soils & Sediments (Day 6, 20 questions) added — Darcy's Law, hydraulic conductivity tests, transmissivity, Dupuit unconfined well, Thiem confined well, specific capacity, retardation factor, vadose zone penetration
Bank now holds 143 questions spanning Days 1–6; every numeric answer recomputed and verified in Python
Cheat sheet extended with 9 new Groundwater equations; printable Day-6 study PDF generated with pdflatex (materials/)
All Day-6 formulas cross-checked against the NCEES FE Reference Handbook v10.6 text (pp. 297–299, 330)
KA 11 (8–12 exam questions) fully covered: subtopics A basic hydrogeology, B groundwater flow, C drawdown, D remediation — SI + USCS mix throughout

history.title: Mark Got/Missed fixed + 25/50% nudges + welcome-back
changes: Fixed: marking a question ✓ Got in the Bank now actually flips it from missed to got (status tracks your latest answer, not whether you ever missed it) and drops it from the Missed list
Encouraging nudges at 25% and 50% of your daily goal, on top of the goal-hit celebration
Gentle 'welcome back' when you return to a study session you left mid-set
New Mock Master badge for scoring 70%+ on a full practice exam (ready for the Jul 3 / Jul 6 practice days)

history.title: Gamification: daily goal, streak protection, badges, focus tracking
changes: Daily goal with a progress ring; meeting it keeps your 🔥 streak — and one missed day a week is forgiven (grace day), with a 🏆 best-streak record
Achievements: 11 unlockable badges (streaks, sections cleared, perfect set, focus, comeback…) shown on the dashboard
Celebrations: confetti + encouraging toasts when you hit your goal, unlock a badge, or ace a set (respects reduced-motion)
Focus tracking: measures real engaged study time and how often you switch away — shown on the dashboard
Set your daily goal in Settings; results now always count toward streak, mastery, and the heatmap

history.title: Dashboard: study calendar + My List / Missed tiles
changes: Study calendar on the dashboard — your whole plan from first set to exam day across the months, with live days in green (click any to review), pending in amber, upcoming dim, exams in red, and today ringed
‘Your study lists’ tiles: ★ My List and ✗ Missed counts with one-click review, plus export-for-Claude
Quick links wire straight into the study player on your saved or missed sets

history.title: Bank: history dots, sorting, My List + mark got/missed
changes: Every Bank card shows a history dot (green = got it, red = missed, grey = not seen yet) — the same read as the study navigator
Sort the bank by day, topic, missed-first, or most-missed
★ My List: star any question to build a custom set; 'Add these to My List' stars the whole current filter; review it with one click
Missed list fills itself automatically; both lists show live counts and open in the study interface
Mark a question ✓ Got or ✗ Missed straight from the card — for when you worked it on paper — and it updates your history, dots, and mastery

history.title: History-aware navigator + collapsible study panels
changes: The question navigator now reflects your saved history: green = got it right, red = ever missed (drill these), grey = not seen yet — so reopening a section shows your progress, not a blank slate
Count legend at the top of the navigator (✓ got · ✗ missed · ○ new)
Show/hide the Questions panel and the Handbook from the study view; the question column reflows to fill the space and your choice is remembered

history.title: Wider layout, day-aware study, dashboard + PDFs
changes: Full-width, fluid layout that uses the whole screen
Today is day-aware — shows the correct day (falls back to the latest available) with a day picker to load any day's full set; plus a question navigator and smooth click-through
Bank: filter by day + topic + mastery; 'Review these →' opens the full study interface on any day, topic, or your misses
Dashboard: publish verification (is today's set live?), clickable schedule with per-day progress
Missed questions tracked + one-click export to paste into Claude as a tutor
New PDFs page: download every daily set, the cheat sheet, and practice exams

history.title: Day 5 added — Surface Water & Hydrology
changes: Surface Water & Hydrology (Day 5, 20 questions) added — Rational Method, SCS/NRCS curve-number runoff, IDF curves, and flood frequency/return-period analysis
Bank now holds 123 questions spanning Days 1-5; every numeric answer recomputed and verified in Python
Cheat sheet (equation index) extended with the Surface Water relations; printable Day-5 study PDF generated with pdflatex (materials/)
Every Day-5 formula cross-checked against the actual NCEES FE Handbook v10.6 text
Auto-published by the daily routine: Today, Bank, and Progress picked up Day 5 from one new day file + manifest line

history.title: Full current bank — 103 questions
changes: Day 2 Set B added (20 more Fluid Mechanics questions) — the bank now holds all 103 questions from your study series so far
End-to-end routine confirmed: dropping one day file + a manifest line flows straight to the live site

history.title: Day 1 added — Days 1-4 fully loaded
changes: Mathematics (Day 1, 18 questions) added — the bank now spans Days 1-4 (83 questions)
Demonstrates the daily routine: one new file + a manifest line, and Today, Bank, and Progress all updated with no code changes

history.title: Real questions Days 2-4 + daily-append structure
changes: 65 real questions now live: Fluid Mechanics I (20), Pipe Flow (21), Hydraulics (24)
New data model: one file per study day (data/days/dayNN.json) + manifest — adding a day auto-updates the whole app
Documented the FE engine to site daily routine (docs/engine-integration.md)

history.title: Real questions, schedule, equations panel
changes: Real exam questions loading from the FE engine, tagged by day/topic
Full equations list in the searchable Handbook panel; KaTeX math
Equations hidden by default; worked solution unlocks step by step; numeric-entry supported

history.title: Equation breakdowns, step-by-step, Handbook panel
changes: Relevant equations shown up front; unlock the worked solution one step at a time
Searchable Handbook panel; Question Bank browse/search/filter; live version badge

history.title: Progress, spaced repetition, settings
changes: Progress dashboard with per-section mastery, streak, days-to-exam
Leitner spaced repetition; Settings: daily size, mix, reveal mode, SRS toggle

history.title: Daily player
changes: Today's daily question set; hide / reveal answers with self-grade; daily streak

history.title: Site live
changes: pefeprep.com live on GitHub Pages with auto-deploy; HTTPS enabled
